content:
# Luke Chapter 2 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), Folios 43v–44v (PDF pages 85–87) | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/LUK_002_page_001.jpg, LUK_002_page_002.jpg, LUK_002_page_003.jpg

**1** And-it-came-to-pass in those days [that] a-decree went-out from Caesar Augustus to-register all the inhabited-[earth].

**2** This registration first came-to-pass [while] Quirinius was-governing Syria.

**3** And all were-going to-be-registered, each to his-own city.

**4** And went-up also Joseph from Galilee, out-of [the] city of-Nazareth, into Judea, to [the] city of-David which is-called Bethlehem, because-of the [fact that] he was of-[the]-house and family of-David,

**5** to-be-registered with Mary the-[one]-having-been-betrothed to-him, being pregnant.

**6** And-it-came-to-pass in the [time of] their being there, the days were-fulfilled for-her to-give-birth.

**7** And she-gave-birth-to the son of-her, the firstborn, and she-wrapped-in-swaddling-cloths him and laid him in a manger, because there-was not a-place for-them in the guest-room.

**8** And shepherds were in the same region, living-in-the-fields and keeping watches of-[the]-night over the flock of-them.

**9** And an-angel of-[the]-Lord stood-upon them, and [the] glory of-[the]-Lord shone-around them, and they-feared [with] a-great fear.

**10** And the angel said to-them: Do-not fear; for behold, I-bring-good-news to-you [of] a-great joy which will-be for-all the people,

**11** that there-was-born to-you today a-Savior, who is Christ [the] Lord, in [the] city of-David.

**12** And this [is] the sign to-you: you-will-find a-baby wrapped-in-swaddling-cloths and lying in a manger.

**13** And suddenly there-came-to-be with the angel a-multitude of-[the]-heavenly host praising God and saying:

**14** Glory in [the] highest [places] to-God, and upon earth peace, in men of-good-will.

**15** And it-came-to-pass, as the angels departed from them into heaven, the shepherds were-speaking to one-another: Let-us-go-through indeed unto Bethlehem and let-us-see this word which has-come-to-pass, which the Lord made-known to-us.

**16** And they-came having-hurried, and they-found both Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.

**17** And having-seen, they-made-known concerning the word which-had-been-spoken to-them about the little-child this.

**18** And all the-[ones]-having-heard marveled concerning the-[things] having-been-spoken by the shepherds to them.

**19** But Mary was-keeping-together all these words, pondering [them] in the heart of-her.

**20** And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God over all [the things] which they-heard and saw, just-as it-was-spoken to them.

**21** And when eight days were-fulfilled to-circumcise him, and his name was-called Jesus, the-[name]-having-been-called by the angel before he was-conceived in the womb.

**22** And when the days of-the purification of-them were-fulfilled according-to the law of-Moses, they-brought him up to Jerusalem to-present [him] to-the Lord —

**23** just-as it-has-been-written in [the] law of-[the]-Lord that: Every male opening a-womb shall-be-called holy to-the Lord —

**24** and to-give a-sacrifice according-to the-[thing]-having-been-said in the law of-[the]-Lord, a-pair of-turtledoves or two young pigeons.

**25** And behold, [there was] a-man in Jerusalem whose name [was] Symeon, and this man [was] righteous and devout, waiting-for [the] consolation of-Israel, and [the] Holy Spirit was upon him.

**26** And it-had-been-revealed to-him by the Holy Spirit, [that he would] not see death before he-should-see the Christ of-[the]-Lord.

**27** And he-came in the Spirit into the temple; and in the [time of] the parents bringing-in the little-child Jesus, for-them to-do according-to the-[thing]-having-been-customary of-the law concerning him,

**28** and he received him into the arms of-him and blessed God and said:

**29** Now you-release the slave of-you, Master, according-to the word of-you, in peace;

**30** because the eyes of-me saw the salvation of-you,

**31** which you-prepared before [the] face of-all the peoples:

**32** a-light for revelation of-[the]-nations, and [the] glory of-[your]-people Israel.

**33** And the father of-him and the mother were marveling at the-[things] being-spoken concerning him.

**34** And Symeon blessed them, and said to Mary the mother of-him: Behold, this-[one] is-set for [the] fall and rising of-many in Israel, and for a-sign being-spoken-against —

**35** and of-you yourself the soul a-sword will-pass-through — so-that [the] thoughts out-of many hearts may-be-revealed.

**36** And [there was] Anna, a-prophetess, daughter of-Phanuel, of-[the]-tribe of-Asher; she [was] advanced in many days, having-lived with a-husband seven years from the virginity of-her,

**37** and she [was] a-widow until eighty-four years, who did-not depart from the temple, serving [with] fastings and prayers night and day.

**38** And she at-the-same hour having-stood-near, was-giving-thanks to-God, and was-speaking concerning him to-all the-[ones] waiting-for [the] redemption of-Jerusalem.

**39** And when they-completed all-[things] according-to the law of-[the]-Lord, they-returned into Galilee, to the city of-themselves, Nazareth.

**40** And the little-child was-growing and being-strengthened, being-filled-with wisdom, and [the] grace of-God was upon him.

**41** And the parents of-him were-going yearly to Jerusalem for-the feast of-the Passover.

**42** And when he became twelve years [old], [with] them going-up according-to the custom of-the feast,

**43** and having-completed the days, in their returning, the boy Jesus remained-behind in Jerusalem, and the parents of-him did-not know.

**44** But having-supposed him to-be in the traveling-company, they-went a-day's journey, and they-were-searching-for him among the relatives and the acquaintances.

**45** And not having-found [him], they-returned to Jerusalem, searching-for him.

**46** And it-came-to-pass after three days, they-found him in the temple, sitting in [the] midst of-the teachers, and hearing them and questioning them.

**47** And all the-[ones]-hearing him were-amazed at the understanding and the answers of-him.

**48** And having-seen him, they-were-astonished, and the mother of-him said to him: Child, why did-you-do to-us thus? Behold, the father of-you and I, being-distressed, were-seeking you.

**49** And he-said to them: Why [is it] that you-were-seeking me? Did-you-not know that in the-[things] of-the Father of-me it-is-necessary [for] me to-be?

**50** And they did-not understand the word which he-spoke to-them.

**51** And he-went-down with them and came to Nazareth, and he-was being-subject to-them. And the mother of-him was-keeping all the words in the heart of-her.

**52** And Jesus was-advancing in wisdom and age and grace with God and men.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 2 — "This registration first came-to-pass"**: Sinaiticus reads ΑΥΤΗ ΑΠΟΓΡΑΦΗ ΠΡΩΤΗ ΕΓΕΝΕΤΟ. The syntax of this verse is notoriously difficult. The word ΠΡΩΤΗ ("first") may function predicatively ("This was the first registration") or adverbially. I have rendered it as closely as possible to the Greek word order.

2. **Verse 5 — "the-[one]-having-been-betrothed"**: Sinaiticus reads ΕΜΝΗΣΤΕΥΜΕΝΗ (betrothed). Some later manuscripts add ΓΥΝΑΙΚΙ ("wife"). Sinaiticus does not include "wife" here.

3. **Verse 7 — "guest-room"**: Greek ΚΑΤΑΛΥΜΑΤΙ. This is not the word for "inn" (ΠΑΝΔΟΧΕΙΟΝ, which appears in Luke 10:34). ΚΑΤΑΛΥΜΑ refers to a guest-room or lodging-place. The traditional rendering "inn" is misleading.

4. **Verse 14 — "in men of-good-will"**: Sinaiticus reads ΕΝ ΑΝΘΡΩΠΟΙΣ ΕΥΔΟΚΙΑΣ (genitive: "of good will/pleasure"), meaning "among men of [God's] good pleasure/favor." This is the reading also found in Vaticanus and the earliest manuscripts. The Byzantine/Majority text reads ΕΥΔΟΚΙΑ (nominative: "good will toward men"), which underlies the KJV rendering. The Sinaiticus genitive reading is almost certainly original.

5. **Verse 22 — "purification of-them"**: Sinaiticus reads ΑΥΤΩΝ ("of them," plural). Some manuscripts read ΑΥΤΟΥ ("of him") or ΑΥΤΗΣ ("of her"). The plural is the reading of Sinaiticus and is retained here.

6. **Verse 26 — "the Christ of-[the]-Lord"**: Sinaiticus reads ΤΟΝ ΧΡΙΣΤΟΝ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ("the Anointed-one of-the-Lord"). This is the standard reading.

7. **Verse 33 — "the father of-him"**: Sinaiticus reads Ο ΠΑΤΗΡ ΑΥΤΟΥ ("the father of him"). Later manuscripts, influenced by the doctrine of the virgin birth, changed this to "Joseph" (ΙΩΣΗΦ) to avoid calling Joseph Jesus' father. The Sinaiticus reading is preserved here as the more difficult and earlier reading.

8. **Verse 36 — "until eighty-four years"**: The Greek is ambiguous: ΕΩΣ ΕΤΩΝ ΟΓΔΟΗΚΟΝΤΑ ΤΕΣΣΑΡΩΝ could mean she was a widow "for" eighty-four years (making her over 100 years old) or "until [the age of]" eighty-four years. I have preserved the preposition literally.

9. **Verse 38 — "redemption of-Jerusalem"**: Sinaiticus reads ΛΥΤΡΩΣΙΝ ΙΕΡΟΥΣΑΛΗΜ ("redemption of Jerusalem"). Some manuscripts read "of Israel" instead.

10. **Verse 43 — "the parents of-him"**: Again Sinaiticus reads ΟΙ ΓΟΝΕΙΣ ΑΥΤΟΥ. Later manuscripts substitute "Joseph and his mother" to protect the virgin birth theology. Sinaiticus is followed here.

11. **Verse 48 — "the father of-you"**: Sinaiticus reads Ο ΠΑΤΗΡ ΣΟΥ. Same pattern as verse 33 — later manuscripts alter this to avoid calling Joseph "father." The Sinaiticus reading is preserved.

12. **Verse 49 — "in the-[things] of-the Father of-me"**: Greek ΕΝ ΤΟΙΣ ΤΟΥ ΠΑΤΡΟΣ ΜΟΥ. This phrase is ambiguous: "in my Father's [house]" or "in my Father's [affairs/business]." The neuter plural article ΤΟΙ can refer to either. I have left it literally as "in the-[things] of" to preserve the ambiguity of the original.

13. **General note on Sinaiticus orthography**: Codex Sinaiticus contains numerous itacistic spellings (ΕΙ for Ι, Η for ΕΙ, etc.) which are regularized in translation without note, as they do not affect meaning.

— EMET
